rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the issue to invalid identifiers or declarations in the same line and fix or remove the problematic clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within AGREE annexes to avoid 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use valid AADL identifiers and types; avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified names.
In implementation scopes, use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables; do not place guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must declare types as int, real, or bool.
Guarantee expressions must have consistent types; avoid mixing bool and <error> types by resolving root references.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or undefined types in AGREE annexes that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assign for bindings.
